description: N+1 query pattern in _slot_type_counts: executes 4 DB queries per lot (1 GROUP BY + 3 per-slot-type queries to check state engine). Called from search_lots (line 44) once per paginated lot. With default limit=100, this fires up to 402 queries for a single endpoint call.

Project-wide: ALL route handlers are async def but use synchronous SQLAlchemy session.query() calls, blocking the asyncio event loop on every DB operation. Every DB call in every endpoint stalls concurrent request processing.

Synchronous joblib.load() in _load_models() called on every request to /predict/occupancy (line 50) and /predict/health (line 69). Loads ML model files (~100s KB each) synchronously, blocking the event loop for tens of milliseconds per request.
Synchronous joblib.load() in _load_agent() called on every request to /pricing/adjust (line 29). Loads RL neural agent file synchronously, blocking the event loop.

Missing DB index on parking_sessions.driver_id — this column is filtered in 6+ endpoints: start_session (line 33), end_session (line 97-98), my_history (line 194), get_session_detail (line 230), session_receipt (line 287), active_sessions (line 174). Each query does a full table scan on a growing table.
Missing DB index on parking_sessions.status — filtered in start_session (line 33), end_session (line 98), active_sessions (line 171). Table scan on every session lifecycle operation.
Missing DB index on transactions.driver_id and transactions.action — filtered in payment history (payments.py:121-123) and join lookup. No driver-level index means full scan for every payment query.

Missing DB index on transactions.timestamp — ORDER BY timestamp DESC in payment history (payments.py:124) and list_transactions (revenue.py:80). Without index, PostgreSQL would sort on every paginated query.
Unbounded fetch: revenue_overview uses .all() without .limit() on RevenueRecord filtered by date >= cutoff. Over 90 days with multiple lots, this returns hundreds of rows with no pagination cap.

Periodic _do_ingest runs every 60 seconds, iterates ALL parking lots, and for each lot calls simulate_ingest() which fires 2 additional DB queries per lot. Synchronous blocking calls in asyncio event loop that can stall concurrent request processing for seconds.

Sequential independent queries in end_session: session lookup (line 97), lot lookup (line 105), and latest occupancy lookup (line 106) each await the previous result but share no data dependency. Could be parallelized.

Sequential independent queries in start_session: lot query (line 44) and recent records query (line 48) share no data dependency. The records query only needs lot_id, which is already in the request, not the query result.
Four sequential independent aggregate queries in revenue_cumulative: sum(RevenueRecord), count(ParkingSession), count(ParkingLot), count(User) executed one after another with no data dependency.

process_pending() called inside request handlers (sessions.py:79, payments.py:95) processes up to 200 pending outbox ledger transactions synchronously, adding blocking blockchain operations to the session start/end/payment request path.

Missing DB index on micro_slots.lot_id — filtered in list_slots (micro.py:90), slot_probability (micro.py:113), list_zones (micro.py:139), reserve_slot (micro.py:160), and seed_slots (micro.py:354). Every micro-slot operation does a full scan.
Missing DB index on micro_slots.micro_zone_id — filtered in list_zones (micro.py:143) via in_() clause. Table scan on every zone listing.
Missing DB index on slot_reservations.driver_id and slot_reservations.status — filtered in release_slot (micro.py:324-325). Full table scan on reservation release.
Missing DB index on prebook_records.driver_id and prebook_records.status — filtered in confirm_prebook (micro.py:264-265, 277-279). Full table scan on prebook confirmation.
SELECT * on OccupancyRecord in get_lot when response only uses 4 of 9 columns (timestamp, occupancy_rate, price, net_flux). Wastes bandwidth for the history array.
SELECT * on Transaction in my_payments when response only uses 6 of 13 columns (tx_hash, lot_id, amount, timestamp, status).
SELECT * on ParkingLot in search_lots when search response only needs ~8 of 12 columns. Also called per-lot with no column projection.
Sequential queries in revenue_overview: queries RevenueRecord (line 41) then queries ParkingLot (line 52) — both independent and could be parallelized via Promise.all or async gather.
Missing DB index on prediction_metrics.timestamp — filtered in server.py cleanup (line 166). Index would speed up the periodic data-retention cleanup query.
Missing DB index on parking_sessions.start_time — used in ORDER BY at sessions.py:196 (my_history). No index causes filesort on paginated history query.
